screenshots.note: Puppeteer unavailable in this environment (dbus socket missing). Visual review conducted from #81 screenshots (screenshot-sofia-desktop-81.png / screenshot-sofia-mobile-81.png). No visual regressions detected in source code since #82.

high_bare_localStorage_strike2.detail: App.jsx lines 1071–1072 still call localStorage.getItem('bloberto-vibe-key') and localStorage.getItem('bloberto-vibe-streak') directly with no try/catch. safeSave/safeRead utilities were added and are now used for writes (lines 1076–1077) and for theme/shortcuts/sparkle reads throughout the file — that partial improvement is real and appreciated. But the two reads at the top of the vibe streak effect remain bare and will throw SecurityError in iOS private browsing, crashing the entire streak logic. Fix: replace lines 1071–1072 with safeRead().

high_handleTabKeyDown_not_useCallback_st.detail: App.jsx line 798: `const handleTabKeyDown = (e) => {` — still a plain inline function. This is recreated on every render of App, causing both tab buttons to see a new onKeyDown reference every time any state changes anywhere in App, defeating React.memo on any component that might receive it and creating unnecessary re-renders. Fix: wrap in useCallback with deps [tab].

suggestions.title: Bare localStorage.getItem in vibe streak effect — Strike 3
suggestions.description:
App.jsx lines 1071–1072 call localStorage.getItem() with no try/catch wrapper. In iOS Safari private browsing, Firefox with strict privacy, and certain enterprise environments, this throws a SecurityError and the entire useEffect callback aborts — the streak is never computed, setVibeStreak is never called, and safeSave on lines 1076–1077 is never reached. The writes below are already using safeSave; the reads need the same treatment.

Fix (2 lines):
  line 1071: const storedKey = safeRead('bloberto-vibe-key')
  line 1072: const storedStreak = parseInt(safeRead('bloberto-vibe-streak') ?? '0', 10)

Note: The vibe history block (lines 1108–1115) is protected by try/catch and therefore low severity despite using bare localStorage — it's inconsistent but safe. Still, for coherence it should be migrated to safeRead/safeSave too.

suggestions.title: handleTabKeyDown not in useCallback — Strike 4
ShortcutToast has no dismiss button for touch/mouse users
suggestions.description:
The ShortcutToast (App.jsx ~line 611) can only be dismissed by: pressing a shortcut key, waiting 7 seconds for auto-dismiss, or it hides itself if a shortcut is used. There is no tap/click dismiss for mobile users or mouse users who can't figure out what keyboard keys to press. On mobile, the toast sits at the bottom right and can obscure content with no way to close it quickly.

From the #81 screenshot, the toast is visible over the office floor. On mobile it overlaps the conference table area.

Fix: Add a small × close button inside the toast div:
  <button className='shortcut-toast__close' onClick={() => dismiss(false)} aria-label='Dismiss keyboard shortcuts hint'>×</button>

Also consider: the toast currently says '⌨️ Shortcuts: 1 Office · 2 Dashboard · R Refresh · Esc Clear' but on touch devices where keyboard shortcuts are irrelevant, maybe suppress the toast entirely (check isTouchDevice).

suggestions.title: Bloberto speech bubble overlaps tab bar on mobile
Vibe history block inconsistently uses bare localStorage inside try/catch
suggestions.description:
App.jsx lines 1108–1115: the vibe history block wraps raw localStorage.getItem / localStorage.setItem in a try/catch. The crash protection is there, which is why this is medium not high. However, the rest of the codebase now uses safeRead/safeSave uniformly, and this block is the only place that deviated from that pattern. It creates a confusing inconsistency when reading the code — a future developer might think the try/catch pattern is intentional and replicate it elsewhere.

Fix: Replace lines 1109 and 1113 with safeRead(VIBE_HISTORY_KEY) and safeSave(VIBE_HISTORY_KEY, JSON.stringify(history)) and remove the try/catch (safeSave/safeRead absorb the exception internally).

suggestions.title: Dead comment: WORKER_CONFETTI_PIECES
suggestions.description:
App.jsx around line 40: `// WORKER_CONFETTI_PIECES removed — worker confetti now uses CONFETTI_PIECES\n// with worker color applied via CSS custom property on the container`

This is a migration-era comment that explains a change that already happened. The code it references no longer exists. It adds noise without adding value — it just explains a ghost.

Fix: Delete the 2-line comment.

suggestions.title: handleCharClick useCallback deps include stable refs
suggestions.description:
Office.jsx handleCharClick useCallback (around line 1279) declares deps: [setPingedId, setPingReaction, pingTimerRef, pingReactionTimerRef, onWorkerClick]. setState setters (setPingedId, setPingReaction) are guaranteed stable by React and should not be in the dep array. Refs (pingTimerRef, pingReactionTimerRef) never change their identity. Including them is harmless but trips lint rules (react-hooks/exhaustive-deps can flag refs as over-specified) and is misleading about what actually gates a re-memoize.

Fix: Trim dep array to [onWorkerClick] — the only non-stable value.

suggestions.title: isTouchDevice computed once at module load — hybrid devices get wrong label
suggestions.description:
App.jsx: `const isTouchDevice = typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches` is evaluated once when the module is first imported. The polling-paused pill then shows 'Tap to refresh' vs 'Move mouse to refresh' based on this snapshot. On hybrid/convertible devices (Surface, iPad with keyboard, etc.) that switch input modes at runtime, the label will be stale if the user switches after page load.

This is low impact but easy to fix. Consider moving the detection inside the component as a `useMemo` or listening to the `change` event on the media query:
  const isTouchDevice = useMemo(() => window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches, [])

Or for live updates: `window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').addEventListener('change', handler)`
